commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Protected maintainer handling plus missing real behavior proof require contributor or maintainer action; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: The diff adds local version metadata reads and tests only; it does not add dependencies, lockfiles, workflow changes, secret handling, downloads, or new third-party execution paths. commands.review_summary:
The branch adds OpenClaw host and Codex plugin build versions to Codex app inventory cache keys and updates focused cache-key tests.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +9. Total +25 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. live repro was established in this read-only review. The source path is clear: current main omits host/plugin versions from the app inventory key that feeds plugin thread-config input fingerprints.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected maintainer handling plus missing real behavior proof require contributor or maintainer action; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: fix(codex): refresh app inventory after upgrades
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Codex app inventory cached by a running OpenClaw process could remain keyed to pre-upgrade behavior after either the OpenClaw host or Codex plugin version changed.

## Why This Change Was Made

The app inventory cache key now includes explicit OpenClaw and Codex plugin build versions alongside the existing runtime identity. The Codex wrapper supplies the host version and resolves the installed plugin package version across source and bundled layouts.

## User Impact

Codex app discovery uses a distinct inventory cache scope after an OpenClaw or Codex plugin upgrade, preventing reuse of inventory associated with a different build.

## Evidence

- Focused Codex cache tests: 2 files passed, 12 tests passed.
- Focused `oxfmt` and `oxlint`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- Autoreview: clean, with no accepted or actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app inventory cache keys must change when either the OpenClaw host version or Codex plugin version changes.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with Node.js 24.14.1, using the production `buildCodexAppInventoryCacheKey` implementation from an isolated `.mem/integ` OpenClaw workspace.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Executed the production TypeScript module through Node.js with otherwise-identical runtime inputs, changing only the OpenClaw host version and then only the Codex plugin version. The executable proof was verified with `uvx showboat verify .mem/integ/proofs/codex-app-inventory-version-key.md`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
{
  "baseline": {
    "openClawVersion": "2026.6.27",
    "codexPluginVersion": "2026.6.10",
    "codexHome": "/isolated/codex",
    "endpoint": null,
    "runtimeIdentity": null,
    "authProfileId": "proof",
    "accountId": null,
    "envApiKeyFingerprint": null,
    "appServerVersion": null
  },
  "hostVersionChangesKey": true,
  "pluginVersionChangesKey": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The serialized production key contains both explicit build versions. Changing only the host version changed the key, and changing only the plugin version changed the key. The Showboat proof replayed successfully; focused Vitest also passed 2 files and 12 tests.
- What was not tested: No live gateway, deployment, devbox, or external Codex app-server was started or modified.
target.author: kevinslin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
